meta.title: Range Rover Sport SV (P635) Engine Replacement Cost UK
meta.description: Every Range Rover Sport SV engine — S68 4.4 V8 at 635 bhp. One generation, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 12 vetted Land Rover specialists today.

Land Rover Range Rover Sport SV (P635) Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover Sport SV (P635) engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned S68 SV-tune engine typically costs £14,000–£18,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. Main dealer new engines can exceed £40,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover Sport SV engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S68 SV-tune is BMW's most powerful production V8 at 635 bhp. It's rated ★★★★☆, but extreme power output brings extreme maintenance demands: hot-vee oil leaks, cooling system vulnerability, and PCV valve issues. No catastrophic bottom-end design flaws have emerged, but this is a high-strung performance engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport SV?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hot-vee oil leaks (valve cover gaskets, turbo oil lines, oil cooler seals), cooling system failures (plastic crossover pipes, water pump), and PCV valve/oil separator issues are the most common. All are repairable if caught early, but prevention is critical given the SV's extreme performance.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport SV engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a £100,000–£140,000+ Range Rover Sport SV, a £14,000–£18,000 recon engine is a manageable 10–15% of the vehicle's value — well worth it. Given the Sport SV's strong retained values and limited production numbers, replacement is almost always viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: For the SV (P635), the engine code is S68 — it's on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The SV badge signifies the 635 bhp twin-turbo V8. If you're unsure, tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the S68's hot-vee oil leaks, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — hot-vee oil leaks are repairable. Valve cover gaskets, turbo oil lines, and oil cooler seals can all be replaced without removing the engine. Early detection is critical — the SV's extreme heat output means oil leaks can damage adjacent electrical components quickly if ignored.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport SV (P635) — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential maintenance — SV runs hotter; catch it before electrical damage
Replace plastic pipes as a preventative measure at 40,000 miles
Straightforward replacement; don't let oil consumption worsen
Replace as a preventative item at 50,000 miles
Not a common S68 failure — if it occurs, replacement is the only option
Repairable by specialists; do not drive with boost issues

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport SV? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with documented oil-change intervals
* Evidence of preventative cooling system maintenance (crossover pipes, water pump)
* Hot-vee oil cooler gaskets replaced as a preventative measure
* Low-mileage example with full running-in service completed
avoidIf: * Service history shows any evidence of oil leaks or coolant top-ups with no diagnosis
* Burning oil smell from the engine bay — potential hot-vee gasket failure
* Low coolant level with no visible leak — potential crossover pipe failure
* Engine warning light present with no diagnosis documentation
* No record of specialist SV maintenance — this is not a standard V8

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover Sport SV's S68 V8 is a masterpiece of engineering — 635 bhp in a luxury SUV. But it's a high-strung performance engine with extreme cooling and oil demands. Buy on service history and proof of preventative cooling system work. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with a 12-month warranty is the only sensible option — dealer new engines can exceed £40,000.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport SV (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle or tick; the S68 should start smoothly and quietly
* Service History — Including which engine era (S68 SV tune is the only SV engine — verify this before purchase)
* Oil Leaks — Check the valley between cylinder banks for oil pooling (hot-vee gasket failure)
* Coolant Level — Is the coolant low with no visible leak? Crossover pipe risk
* Burning Oil Smell — Any smell from the engine bay after a drive? Hot-vee oil leak indicator
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 2,000 miles? If so, investigate PCV/oil separator
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved cooling or fuelling issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; the S68 should pass easily if well-maintained
